title: Fix formatting real to binary and hex strings on ARM
body:
Directly casting a negative double to a unsigned integer type is undefined behavior and has different results on x86 and ARM based platforms.

On x86 the behavior is similar to casting a signed int to an unsigned integer, i.e. the sign bit will end up in the MSB. But on ARM the result will be 0.

To get consistent behavior, first cast to signed integer and then cast the signed integer value to an unsigned integer value.

Resolves #917
